stem: A retail study measures customer satisfaction score across 3 chains, each containing multiple stores. chain A has 8 observations with mean 71.6 /10 (within-chain SD = 3.6). chain B has 20 observations with mean 72.8 /10 (within-chain SD = 3.8). chain C has 10 observations with mean 73.4 /10 (within-chain SD = 7.7). The between-chain standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 4.0 and 9.9 /10. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true customer satisfaction score for chain B, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-chain variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for chain B: 72.8 /10 (shrunk from raw 72.8). Range [72.8, 72.89999999999999] as between-chain SD varies from 4.0 to 9.9. Pooled mean: 72.7.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 72.7 /10 (weighted average of 3 chain means).
Shrinkage factor for chain B: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/20). At σ_between=6.6: B = 0.984.
Interval [72.8, 72.89999999999999] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (72.8) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (72.7) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
